minutes_briefs.title: BC Shared Health Services added to Health Care Employers Regulation
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has added 'BC Shared Health Services' to the list of employers covered by the Health Care Employers Regulation. This means BC Shared Health Services is now officially part of the group of public-sector health care employers that must follow the rules in this regulation. The change was made through an Order in Council (a formal government order) and took effect on April 20, 2026.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No action needed from small business owners — this change only affects public-sector health care employers.
minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 60/2026
Made under: Public Sector Employers Act
Published in: No. 7 – B.C. Regs. 57 to 61, April 21, 2026
Deposit date: April 20, 2026

B.C. Reg. 60/2026, deposited April 20, 2026, under thePUBLIC SECTOR EMPLOYERS ACT[section 15]. Order in Council 151/2026, approved and ordered April 20, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that section 2 of the Health Care Employers Regulation, B.C. Reg. 427/94, is amended by adding "BC Shared Health Services". — B. BAILEY,Minister of Finance; L. POPHAM,Presiding Member of the Executive Council.
